utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 5 items required in the format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. The final coverage plan includes all 5 items by name. Now check if each is fully specified with all required details: 1) health_coverage requires physical and mental health insurance with costs. The plan includes description and costs: base_premium €160/month, added premiums with amounts, and estimated total €235–€260/month; includes mental health via wellness programs and assessments. This is fully specified. 2) property_coverage requires home/contents insurance with costs. The plan includes a description but no concrete costs; notes say exact figures to be refined. Not fully specified. 3) travel_coverage requires travel insurance with costs. The plan provides description but no costs; only notes about bundling. Not fully specified. 4) additional_services requires any enhancement services with costs. The plan lists services but provides no costs. Not fully specified. 5) total_budget requires complete monthly/annual cost breakdown. The plan only provides a partial monthly estimate tied to health and states final budgets TBD; no annual breakdown. Not fully specified. Therefore, 1 out of 5 items is fully specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: From the final coverage plan: Health coverage explicitly includes LifeFirst Standard Plan and InsurePlus Standard Plan as the core. It also mentions add-ons like Hypertensive Emergency Care, Crisis Response Enhancement, and Wellness Programs; among these, only Wellness Programs appears in the ground-truth list with a rating. Property coverage references EliteHome Premium Coverage by name. Travel coverage references PrimeTravel Premium Plan by name. Additional services mention Insurance Portfolio Management service, which is listed in general_services with a rating. Therefore, we map these named items to their exact ratings from the ground-truth.
